qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded batch endpoint allows denial-of-service via unlimited parallel sub-requests
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable endpoint and root cause: this finding concerns unbounded POST /batch sub-request fan-out via Promise.all causing availability degradation, while the advisory concerns GET /users/me leaking unsanitized auth data because a master-level session query context reaches user-data sanitization.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying final finding identifies the advisory-described /users/me auth-data exposure, master-auth context leak, auth adapter sanitization bypass, or equivalent source-to-sink condition. The only qualifying final finding is an unrelated /batch endpoint resource-exhaustion issue.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-37mj-c2wf-cx96 describes authenticated GET /users/me returning unsanitized auth data, including MFA TOTP secrets and recovery codes, because the endpoint uses master-level authentication for the session query and leaks that context into user data.
batch-endpoint-dos/vulnerability_report.json identifies POST /batch, src/batch.js:136-157, and Promise.all over an unbounded requests array as the vulnerable operation.
logs/orchestrator_final_response.md lists batch-endpoint-dos as the only qualifying final finding and ssrf-webhook-poc as non_qualifying_vuln.
